comment.body:
Also don't forget, that I said "temporal squeeze". So yesterday the market could manage to hold aboe my marked 5652 lvl, and so the downside hedges could not print, they decayed providing us a tailwind. 
When I saw vol gets smashed, I signaled you long at 5667. However the market still wants down.  

The models say that while the spot is constantly declining the data series (intraday price actions over the past weeks), still tend to revert to its mean, rather than enterin an explosive vol regime. But the regime is about to shifting according to the model.

comment.body:
Listen now, to me this was the main indicator to call that we get a temporal squeeze. Bcs if market would have been triggered into that put hedge territory, we could have got an explosive move down. 
And that would have been too early for the insiders who want sustained, controlled slowdown, according to my last weekend post. So they had to save the market from that. 
And this, along with the vol crush at 5666/70 range made me confident, that manipulation is going on at max. 
So they can mess with the metrics and algos. 
We saw this back in 2022.

comment.body:
Hallo, happy sunday,
I'll be here late PM and write the post.
Now, i just want to share two videos with you for educational purposes:
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=7yK-5vOR74c
and
https://www.wqed.org/watch/frontline/frontline-catch-trader/

the first one is an analysis of the last year derisking effect from Charlie McElligott from Nomura,
the second one is a documentary about insider trading. 
have fun, and enjoy the spring

comment.body:
interesting podcast with Charlie McElligott and his perspective on how he looks at markets 
the short vol trade was so crowded cause of the recency bias on a multiyear look back
Did it reveal the fragility of the situation?

comment.body:
Hallo, happy sunday,
I'll be here late PM and write the post.
Now, i just want to share two videos with you for educational purposes:
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=7yK-5vOR74c
and
https://www.wqed.org/watch/frontline/frontline-catch-trader/

the first one is an analysis of the last year derisking effect from Charlie McElligott from Nomura,
the second one is a documentary about insider trading. 
have fun, and enjoy the spring

comment.body:
have you read the post? i wrote, '24s april low potential
but it is pointless to guess, bcs this regime is full of 0DTE manipulation that you can't see forward, bcs those are new positions during the day.
very high intraday activity, increased negative gamma, sticky high vol, fat left-tail, very sensitive environment.
this is why i keep saying, in this regime you go day by day. 
i traded along these regimes many times. ;))

comment.body:
by the way, i see this mistake many times...
guys, the market just opened. you haven't seen anything yet. it is highly volatile, you got no positioning, no momentum, no pattern for this week. 
why would you like to chase any trades only bcs it is opened? :))
in these regimes, one better just watch first before acting. 
Be patient, 
trade only when you got a read on the tape, and got a plan on that read.
don't trade without concept, or they OWN you.
this is the most common and worse mistake traders keep commiting.
don't follow them. wait for the market to give it to you. okay?

Read my post, digest it, do your research on that, and listen patiently.
and the market will come to you when it doesn't know, and then you jump on it like a lion on its prey, and run away with the money

comment.body:
:))
thank you.
yeah thats a crucial content to understand.
it's not like a conteo like MMs hunting stop-losses and stuff, but it is something that actually exist and we have to take that into account sometimes. 
for now, they aggressively drive the market. this is what i recognized on that monday thats why i keep linking that to read through the breakdown... 
but it is not always like that. 
for now, it is. 
the main basis is the economic cold-war.

comment.body:
here is the definition of beta. https://stochvoltrader.substack.com/p/the-concept-of-risk-var-beta-sharpe

a bit like correlation, but beta tells you the MAGNITUDE of change in one variable relative to the other, not simply correlation. vol adjusted correlation if you like it so
